code: @Article{,   title = {consexpressionR: an R package for consensus differential gene expression analysis},   author = {Juliana Costa-Silva and David Menotti and Fabricio M. Lopes},   year = {2025},   journal = {arxiv},   doi = {10.48550/arXiv.2503.21546}, }

what: Differential Expression Analysis using consensus of 7 methods
title: Differential Expression Analysis using consensus of 7 methods
text: R package consexpressionR R package differential expression analysis count quantification data. just one count quantification file, user can obtain list genes indicated differentially expressed 5 methods. user-friendly graphical interface, can used web browser, user can parameterize 7 expression analysis methodologies. differential expression analysis performed methods edgeR, DeSeq2, SAMSeq, KnowSeq, EBSeq, limma, NOISeq. results tool can written .csv files. Visualization carried heat map Plot, containing genes considered differentially expressed 5 methods. consexpressionR R package differential expression analysis count quantification data. just one count quantification file, user can obtain list genes indicated differentially expressed 5 methods. user-friendly graphical interface, can used web browser, user can parameterize 7 expression analysis methodologies. differential expression analysis performed methods edgeR, DeSeq2, SAMSeq, KnowSeq, EBSeq, limma, NOISeq. results tool can written .csv files. Visualization carried radarPlot, containing genes thatwere considered differentially expressed 5 methods. can see pkgdown action https://costasilvati.shinyapps.io/consexpressionRapp/. Learn vignette("consexpressionR").

title: Differential Expression Analysis using consensus of 7 methods
code: # Install released version from github library(devtools) install_github("costasilvati/consexpressionR") library(consexpressionR)

title: Differential Expression Analysis using consensus of 7 methods
text: run shiny aplication view consexpressionR browser:
code: # Install released version from github library(devtools) install_github("costasilvati/consexpressionR") library(consexpressionR) # shinyApp running in localhost consexpressionR() library(consexpressionR) # shinyApp running in localhost consexpressionR()

code: data(gse95077) treats <- c("BM", "JJ") cons_result <- runExpression(numberReplics = 3,                               groupName = treats,                               rDataFrameCount = gse95077, thresholds... #> Getting number of false positives in the permutation... #> Differential expression analysis completed. expDef_result <- expressionDefinition(resultTool = cons_result, what: consexpressionR: Differential Expression Analysis using consensus of multiple methods — consexpressionR
title: consexpressionR: Differential Expression Analysis using consensus of multiple methods — consexpressionR
text: Comprehensive workflow differential expression (DE) analysis using multiple methods (e.g., DESeq2, edgeR, limma, EBSeq, NOISeq, SAMSeq, KnowSeq) consensus strategies derive robust DE gene lists.

title: consexpressionR: Differential Expression Analysis using consensus of multiple methods — consexpressionR
text: Maintainer: Juliana Costa-Silva julianacostasilvati@gmail.com (ORCID) [funder] Authors: David Menotti menottid@gmail.com (ORCID) Fabricio M. Lopes fabricio@utfpr.edu.br (ORCID)

title: consexpressionR: Differential Expression Analysis using consensus of multiple methods — consexpressionR
code: obj <- createExpressionResultSet(   results = list(edgeR = data.frame(gene = "g1", logFC = 1)),   methodNames = "edgeR" ) obj #> ExpressionResultSet object #> Methods executed: edgeR #> Result tables: 1 #> Consensus: not computed or empty.

text: Constructor ExpressionResultSet

title: Constructor for ExpressionResultSet — createExpressionResultSet
code: createExpressionResultSet(   results,   methodNames,   parameters = list(),   consensus = list() )

title: Constructor for ExpressionResultSet — createExpressionResultSet
text: results Named list data.frames (NULL elements). methodNames Character vector method names. parameters List analysis parameters. consensus List consensus results (default: empty list).

title: Constructor for ExpressionResultSet — createExpressionResultSet
text: ExpressionResultSet object.

title: Constructor for ExpressionResultSet — createExpressionResultSet
code: res_list <- list(   edgeR = data.frame(gene = c("g1","g2"), logFC = c(1.2, -0.4)),   DESeq2 = NULL )  obj <- createExpressionResultSet(   results = res_list,   methodNames = c("edgeR", "DESeq2"),   parameters = list(alpha = 0.05),   consensus = list(g1 = TRUE) )  obj #> ExpressionResultSet object #> Methods executed: edgeR, DESeq2 #> Result tables: 2 #> Consensus: available (1 gene(s)) summary(obj) #>              Length               Class                Mode  #>                   1 ExpressionResultSet                  S4

what: Identify DE genes by method-specific thresholds — expressionDefinition
title: Identify DE genes by method-specific thresholds — expressionDefinition
text: Applies specific filtering rules expression method result ExpressionResultSet object returns DE genes per tool.

title: Identify DE genes by method-specific thresholds — expressionDefinition
code: expressionDefinition(   resultTool,   groups = c(""),   lfcMinLimma = -2,   lfcMaxLimma = 2,   pValueLimma = 0.05,   FLimma = 0.8,   lfcMinSamseq = -2,   lfcMaxSamseq = 2,   qValueSamseq = 0.05,   scoreDSamseq = 0.8,   lfcMinDeseq2 = -2,   lfcMaxDeseq2 = 2,   pValueDeseq2 = 0.05,   lfcMinEdger = -2,   lfcMaxEdger = 2,   pValueEdger = 0.05,   probNoiseq = 0.8,   lfcMaxKnowseq = 2,   lfcMinKnowseq = -2,   pValueKnowseq = 0.05,   deClassEbseq = "DE",   ppThresholdEbseq = 0.8,   printResults = FALSE,   pathOutput = "." )

title: Identify DE genes by method-specific thresholds — expressionDefinition
text: resultTool ExpressionResultSet object. groups Character vector group names conditions. lfcMinLimma Minimum logFC threshold limma. lfcMaxLimma Maximum logFC threshold limma. pValueLimma P-value cutoff limma. FLimma Minimum F-statistic multi-group limma. lfcMinSamseq Minimum fold-change SAMSeq. lfcMaxSamseq Maximum fold-change SAMSeq. qValueSamseq q-value cutoff SAMSeq. scoreDSamseq Score(d) threshold SAMSeq multi-class. lfcMinDeseq2 Minimum log2 fold-change DESeq2. lfcMaxDeseq2 Maximum log2 fold-change DESeq2. pValueDeseq2 P-value cutoff DESeq2. lfcMinEdger Minimum logFC edgeR. lfcMaxEdger Maximum logFC edgeR. pValueEdger P-value cutoff edgeR. probNoiseq Probability threshold NOISeq. lfcMaxKnowseq Maximum logFC KnowSeq. lfcMinKnowseq Minimum logFC KnowSeq. pValueKnowseq P-value cutoff KnowSeq. deClassEbseq DE class filter EBSeq (e.g. "DE" "EE"). ppThresholdEbseq Posterior probability threshold EBSeq. printResults Logical. Whether write results disk. pathOutput Character. Directory path save result tables.

title: Identify DE genes by method-specific thresholds — expressionDefinition
text: list data.frames DE genes per method.

title: Identify DE genes by method-specific thresholds — expressionDefinition

controlDeseq2 = "BM",                      contrastDeseq2 = "JJ" ) #> calcNormFactors has been renamed to normLibSizes #> Using classic mode. #> Getting annotation of the Homo Sapiens... #> Using title: Read table count File and creates a matrix from the given content file. — readCountFile
code: # Example using a small toy count table tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".csv") write.csv(   data.frame(     gene = c("gene1", "gene2"),     sample1 = c(10, 20),     sample2 = c(15, 25)   ),   tmp,   row.names = FALSE ) counts <- readCountFile(tmp) head(counts) #>       sample1 sample2 #> gene1      10      15 #> gene2      20      25

title: Execute EBSeq gene Expression analysis — runEbseq
text: countMatrix either matrix raw (read) counts. designExperiment replicate treatment samples fdr parameter used EBTest function: fdr False Discovery Rate cutt ppThreshold posterior Probability Threshold maxRound parameter used EBTest function: Number iterations. default value 50. methodDeResults parameter used GetDEResults function: "robust" "classic". Using "robust" option, EBSeq robust genes outliers genes extremely small variances. Using "classic" option, results comparable obtained using GetPPMat() function earlier version (<= 1.7.0) EBSeq groups text, name samples treatment

title: Make expression analysis of multiple tools and return results by tool. — runExpression
code: runExpression(   numberReplics,   groupName,   tableCountPath = "data/gse95077.csv",   sepCharacter = ",",   rDataFrameCount = NULL,   experimentName = "genericExperiment",   outDirPath = tempdir(),   printResults = FALSE,   fitTypeDeseq2 = "local",   controlDeseq2 = "",   contrastDeseq2 = "",   methodNormLimma = "TMM",   methodAdjPvalueLimma = "BH",   numberTopTableLimma = 1e+06,   filterIdKnowseq = "ensembl_gene_id",   notSapiensKnowseq = FALSE,   methodNormEdgeR = "TMM",   normNoiseq = "rpkm",   kNoiseq = 0.5,   factorNoiseq = "Tissue",   lcNoiseq = 0,   replicatesNoiseq = "technical",   condExpNoiseq = c(""),   respTypeSamseq = "Two class unpaired",   npermSamseq = 100,   fdrEbseq = 0.05,   maxRoundEbseq = 50,   methodDeResultsEbseq = "robust",   deNovoAanalysis = FALSE,   progressShiny = NULL )

title: Make expression analysis of multiple tools and return results by tool. — runExpression
text: numberReplics number replicates (technical biological) per group groupName character vector, group/treatment names (e.g., c("BM","JJ")) tableCountPath path csv file contains count/abundance data (local) sepCharacter separator used split csv data (comma tab) rDataFrameCount data.frame/matrix table count; rownames genes colnames samples experimentName experiment name outDirPath output directory (created needed) printResults TRUE, writes per-tool outputs outDirPath fitTypeDeseq2 DESeq2 fitType controlDeseq2 control group label DESeq2 contrastDeseq2 contrast group label DESeq2 methodNormLimma normalization method used limma/edgeR methodAdjPvalueLimma p-value adjustment method limma numberTopTableLimma max number genes limma topTable filterIdKnowseq KnowSeq parameter notSapiensKnowseq KnowSeq parameter methodNormEdgeR normalization method edgeR normNoiseq NOISeq parameter kNoiseq NOISeq parameter factorNoiseq NOISeq parameter lcNoiseq NOISeq parameter replicatesNoiseq NOISeq parameter condExpNoiseq NOISeq parameter respTypeSamseq SAMSeq parameter npermSamseq SAMSeq parameter fdrEbseq EBSeq parameter maxRoundEbseq EBSeq parameter methodDeResultsEbseq EBSeq parameter deNovoAanalysis TRUE, skips KnowSeq (requires annotation) progressShiny optional shiny progress callback

what: Run differential expression analysis with KnowSeq — runKnowSeq
title: Run differential expression analysis with KnowSeq — runKnowSeq
text: Executes two-group differential expression analysis using functions KnowSeq package.

title: Run differential expression analysis with KnowSeq — runKnowSeq
code: runKnowSeq(   count,   groupName,   numberReplic,   filterId = "ensembl_gene_id",   notSapiens = FALSE )

title: Run differential expression analysis with KnowSeq — runKnowSeq
text: count matrix data.frame raw counts abundance values, genes rows samples columns. groupName character vector exactly two group labels. numberReplic single positive integer indicating number replicates per group. filterId single character string specifying annotation filter used KnowSeq::getGenesAnnotation(). notSapiens single logical value indicating whether non-human annotation dataset used.

title: Run differential expression analysis with KnowSeq — runKnowSeq
text: data.frame containing differential expression results returned KnowSeq::DEGsExtraction().

title: Run differential expression analysis with KnowSeq — runKnowSeq
text: function wraps main KnowSeq workflow steps: retrieve gene annotation KnowSeq::getGenesAnnotation(); compute expression values KnowSeq::calculateGeneExpressionValues(); extract differentially expressed genes KnowSeq::DEGsExtraction(). function expects exactly two groups. Row names count must contain gene identifiers compatible selected filterId. workflow depends external package behavior may require internet access, errors raised internal KnowSeq, cqn, mclust calls rethrown additional context.

title: Run differential expression analysis with KnowSeq — runKnowSeq
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ data(gse95077) treats <- c("BM", "JJ") res <- runKnowSeq(   count = gse95077,   groupName = treats,   numberReplic = 3 ) head(res) } # }

what: Execute NOISeq gene Expression analysis — runNoiSeq
title: Execute NOISeq gene Expression analysis — runNoiSeq
text: Execute NOISeq gene Expression analysis

title: Execute NOISeq gene Expression analysis — runNoiSeq
code: runNoiSeq(   countMatrix,   designExperiment,   groups = c(""),   normParm = "rpkm",   kParam = 0.5,   factorParam = c("Tissue"),   lcParam = 0,   replicatesParam = "technical",   condExp = c("") )

title: Execute NOISeq gene Expression analysis — runNoiSeq
text: countMatrix either matrix raw (read) counts. designExperiment replicate treatment samples. groups text list separated comma, name samples treatment. normParm Normalization method t can one "rpkm" (default), "uqua" (upper quartile), "tmm" (trimmed mean M) "n" (normalization). kParam Counts equal 0 replaced k. default, k = 0.5 factorParam string indicating name factor whose levels conditions compared. lcParam Length correction done dividing expression length^lc. default, lc = 0 replicatesParam argument, type replicates used defined: "technical", "biological" "" replicates. default, "technical" replicates option chosen. condExp vector containing two conditions compared differential expression algorithm (needed factor contains 2 different conditions).

title: Execute NOISeq gene Expression analysis — runNoiSeq
text: data.frame output noiseqOut@results
